game_info.title: Old Man's Journey
game_info.concept: **Old Man's Journey:** Players guide an old man across whimsical landscapes by manipulating the terrain directly. The core mechanic involves tapping and dragging on specific, highlighted terrain lines to raise or lower sections of the environment, creating continuous paths for the old man to walk on. The old man automatically walks along any connected path at a constant speed of approximately 2 units per second, requiring players to strategically adjust 2-4 overlapping terrain layers to bridge gaps or create descents. Puzzles escalate in complexity by introducing more intricate multi-layered landscapes and interactive elements like movable objects or animals that also require pathing. Progression is linear, advancing through distinct narrative scenes upon successfully guiding the old man to the next objective, with no explicit failure state, focusing on exploration and puzzle solving.
game_info.description: Guide an old man through beautiful landscapes by shaping the terrain beneath his feet. The old man walks automatically along connected paths. Your task is to manipulate terrain layers by dragging them up or down to create continuous walkable surfaces. Each level presents a puzzle where you must adjust multiple overlapping terrain sections to help the old man reach his destination. There is no failure - take your time to explore and solve each scenic puzzle at your own pace.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys (←/→): Select terrain layer to manipulate
Arrow Keys (↑/↓): Raise or lower selected terrain layer
Space: Confirm terrain adjustment and let the old man walk
R: Restart current level
Enter: Start game / Continue to next level
ESC: Pause/Unpause game

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic terrain manipulation and old man movement. This test verifies that the player can select different terrain layers, adjust their heights, and that the old man successfully walks along the created path. It ensures the core mechanic of the game works correctly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: The test will automatically select the first terrain layer, raise it to connect with adjacent terrain, then select the next layer and adjust it similarly. After each adjustment, it will use Space to confirm and allow the old man to progress. The test continues manipulating terrain systematically until the old man reaches the goal.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The test is successful when the old man reaches the destination point after all terrain adjustments. The terrain layers should visibly move up and down in response to inputs, the old man should walk smoothly along connected paths, and the game should transition to the next level or victory screen upon completion.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing rapid level progression and win condition. This test accelerates through multiple levels by quickly solving terrain puzzles to verify that level transitions work correctly and the game reaches a proper win state. It validates the progression system and end-game handling.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The test succeeds when it completes all levels and triggers the game's win condition. Each level should transition smoothly to the next, the old man should consistently reach each goal, and the final screen should display a victory message. No errors or stuck states should occur during rapid progression.
